name: when-bridging-web-cli-use-web-cli-teleport
description: Bridge web interfaces with CLI workflows for seamless bidirectional integration
capabilities: Build REST API endpoints for CLI command execution
Create web interface components to trigger CLI commands
Deploy bridge server with Express and Socket.io
author: DNYoussef
securitySummary: This is documentation/guidance skill containing no malicious code. It provides example patterns for web-CLI integration with explicit security warnings. The static analyzer flagged example code patterns (exec, child_process) that are documented educational examples with acknowledged security considerations. The skill itself documents these risks and provides security best practices.
valueStatement: Users need a way to integrate web applications with command-line tools for real-time communication. This skill provides step-by-step guidance for building bidirectional web-CLI bridges using REST APIs and WebSockets.
userTitle: Bridge web interfaces with CLI workflows
limitations: Skill provides guidance only, not executable code
Requires manual implementation of security measures
No built-in authentication or command whitelisting

useCases.title: Remote CLI Control
useCases.description: Control CLI tools from a web dashboard for easier DevOps management and monitoring

useCases.title: Build System Integration
useCases.description: Trigger builds and deployments from web interfaces with real-time output streaming

useCases.title: Admin Dashboard
useCases.description: Create web-based admin panels for server management and command execution

promptTemplates.title: Basic Bridge Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a web-CLI bridge that allows a React web interface to execute simple CLI commands and display output via WebSocket

promptTemplates.title: API Design
promptTemplates.prompt: Design REST API specification for a web-CLI bridge with endpoints for command execution and real-time output streaming

promptTemplates.title: Security Hardening
promptTemplates.prompt: Add authentication, command whitelisting, and input validation to the web-CLI bridge to prevent unauthorized command execution

promptTemplates.title: Production Deployment
promptTemplates.prompt: Create Docker deployment configuration and monitoring setup for a production web-CLI bridge server

bestPractices: Implement authentication and command whitelisting before deployment
Validate and sanitize all user inputs to prevent command injection
Use rate limiting and CORS restrictions to protect API endpoints
antiPatterns: Using exec() with unsanitized user input directly
Leaving CORS open to all origins in production

faq.question: Which AI tools support this skill?
faq.answer: This skill works with Claude, Claude Code, and Codex for generating bridge components and server implementations.

faq.question: What are the performance targets?
faq.answer: API response under 200ms, WebSocket latency under 50ms, command execution under 5 seconds, uptime above 99.9%.

faq.question: Can I use this with existing CLI tools?
faq.answer: Yes, the bridge can wrap any CLI tool by sending commands through the REST API or WebSocket connection.

faq.question: How do I secure the bridge server?
faq.answer: Implement authentication, validate all inputs, whitelist allowed commands, restrict CORS origins, and add rate limiting.

faq.question: What if commands hang or timeout?
faq.answer: Implement timeout mechanisms for long-running commands and use async execution with proper error handling.

faq.question: How is this different from SSH?
faq.answer: This provides web-based access without SSH infrastructure. It is designed for controlled, API-driven command execution with a graphical interface.
